channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Number 53 Brougham Road’s front garden is overflowing, 11th March
item.description:
With litter. I saw a rat there and a fox. The litter is knee high. It’s truly awful and blowing into the street. Is this house abandoned? Is there person inside vulnerable and needs help? I think it’s an environmental health issue to clear it. Thank you
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brougham Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Area of potholes, 11th March
item.description:
Various potholes in the middle of the road by crombie

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 336, Old Farm Avenue, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Report of Abandoned Vehicles & Highway Nuisance, 11th March
item.description:
I am reporting multiple vehicles (5+) left in a state of disrepair (flat tyres & crash damage) While some vehicles are taxed, they meet the criteria for abandonment under the Refuse Disposal (Amenity) Act 1978 due to their condition (missing panels, crash damage, and flat tyres). National guidance (and Bromley's own policy) confirms that a vehicle can be deemed abandoned even if it has valid tax if it appears neglected or hazardous.. Furthermore, their condition (damage, leaking fluids) constitutes a public nuisance and danger under Section 149 of the Highways Act 1980. This is not a parking dispute; it is a case of a commercial entity using the public highway for the long-term storage of unroadworthy scrap; which also constitutes a breach of the Clean Neighbourhoods and Environment Act 2005 Please confirm when an inspector will attend to assess these for removal.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kent House Lane, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dumped rubbish / flytipping, 11th March
item.description:
At the start of Thornton Road on the corner that connects to Gladeswood Road there is rubbish that has been flytipped / dumped on the pavement. It looks like used bedding and a bucket piled up. It is unclear whether there is anything hazourdous there.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Thornton Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cable across from of propertylies, 11th March
item.description:
Whilst I appreciate this is not on the pavement I caught my foot in this what looks like an electric cable when walking. Very very dangerous. Needs being under the ground.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Avery Hill Road, Avery Hill
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Foliage part blocking public footpath., 11th March
item.description:
This problem was reported on 6/8/25 and yet to be rectified. Between 38 St Ann's Road and the railway bridge on the south side of St Ann's Road, foliage from behind the fence has part blocked the pavement. In addition, it is obstructing a road sign from being seen by pedestrians, cyclists and road traffic. In the attached photo, the base of the pole can be seen with the blue sign barely visible. The two other photos show the obstruction from both sides. The foliage needs to be trimmed back to the fence and any overhanging foliage removed to a height of two metres to make the sign visible and enable the full width of the pavement to be used.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St Ann's Road, West Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Faded height signs at elevation of bridge, 11th March
item.description:
Hi, I would like to bring to your attention the extremely faded height warning signs, which require replacing, on the brick arch span of Saw Mill Viaduct over Crayford Creek Road, just off the A206.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Barnes Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The whole street needs to be cleaned, 11th March
item.description:
Lots of rubbish all over the street
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Moresby Road, Hackney, London

item.title: 3 oil cans (full) on Ray Lamb Way, 11th March
item.description:
3 full containers of oil have been abandoned on the pavement on Ray Lamb Way. a local motorist stopped and told me it had been there since yesterday.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ray Lamb Way, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Cracks in wall, 11th March
item.description:
Walk opposite Betts park playground has cracks and holes in it and looks precarious and dangerous

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Seymour Villas, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large items fly tipped, 11th March
item.description:
A number of large items including mattresses have been dumped onto bike racks around the E9 5LN post code.

item.title: Cables/Wires, 11th March
item.description:
A report was made several days ago about wires and cables dumped over the grass verge on Ray Lamb Way. An email was recieved yesterday (10/2) claiming the issue had been 'fixed'. This morning the issue is very much unchanged. Photo shows email and new photo of issue 11/2.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ray Lamb Way, Slade Green, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged Lime Bike, 11th March
item.description:
This needs removing as it is damaged and blocks the pavement.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1-24, Luxford Street, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unsafe building work, 11th March
item.description:
There has been building work going on at 27 Camborne Road for many months. It is all very rickety and the rainwater from the house has been diverted via an unstable guttering onto Shoulder of Mutton Green all this time causing a flooded area in times of heavy rain. The barriers surrounding the house is waving about in the wind making it unsafe. The entire building site is a complete mess and shows no sign of being finished. Please investigate.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Camborne Road, Falconwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
